figures.caption: Figure 2: Architecture of MSTCN-CA with two TMS blocks, each using four parallel depthwise dilated convolutions (d=1,2,4,8) and channel attention.

Table 1: Taxonomy of model architectures by Channel-Interaction strategy. Different state-of-the-art models align with the established Channel Interaction (CI, CD, CP) strategies. RhyMix is positioned as a hybrid design (CI+CD) via parallel Cyclic and MSTCN-CA Paths with adaptive gating to capture multi-scale dynamics. (Asym.: Asymmetry, Lag.: Lagginess, Pol.: Polarity, Gw.: group-wise, Dyn.: Dynamism, Ms.: Multi-scale).
Table 2: Comparison of architectural characteristics. Evaluation of RhyMix against CI, CD, and CP channel interaction strategies.
Table 3: Benchmark Datasets for Long-Term Forecasting.

Table 4: Multivariate long-term forecasting results. Input look-back window L = 96. Prediction horizons H ∈ {96, 192, 336, 720}. Avg is averaged from all four prediction lengths. Red: 1st rank, Blue: 2nd rank. We evaluate each model five times and report the mean (standard deviation); results marked with underline are not significantly worse than the first rank (Wilcoxon signed-rank test with Holm–Bonferroni correction, α = 0.05). Example: 0.010(0.001) means 0.010 ± 0.001. TimeM.: TimeMixer++, Impr.: Improvement %.

Table 5: Long-term forecasting evaluation results on PEMS datasets. Prediction horizons H ∈ {12, 24, 48, 96}. Red/Blue: First/Second ranks. We evaluate each model five times and report the mean (standard deviation); results marked with underline are not significantly worse than the first rank (Wilcoxon signed-rank test with Holm–Bonferroni correction, α = 0.05). Example: 0.010(0.001) means 0.010 ± 0.001. Tra.: Transformer, BiL.: BiLSTM, iTrans.: iTransformer, M.TCN: ModernTCN, Impr.: Improvement %.
Figure 5: Visualization of Weather dataset predictions by different models under the input-96-predict-336 setting.
Figure 6: Visualization of ECL dataset predictions by different models under the input-96-predict-336 setting.
Figure 7: Impact of input length L on MSE across ETTh1, ETTh2, ETTm1, and Weather dataset.
Figure 8: Wave representations at key stages of RhyMix (On ETTh1 dataset, C = 7)
Figure 9: Channel mixer: input-to-output wave visualization (On ETTh1 dataset, C = 7).
Figure 10: Reshaping a 1D time series (L=96) into a 2D image with period P=12. Col-Patterns (horizontal) reveal seasonality; Row-Patterns (vertical) reveal trend on the ETTh1 dataset.
Figure 11: RhyMix: Vertical Row Patterns (Trend) and Horizontal Col Patterns (Seasonality) on the ETTh1 dataset at three scales (L=192, 96, 48) using periods P=12, 24, 48, 168.
Table 6: Ablation study on ETTh1, ETTh2, ETTm1, Weather, and ECL datasets (H ∈ {96, 192, 336, 720}). Red/Blue: First/Second ranks. w/o A. Gates: Adaptive Gates
Table 7: Ablation study on PEMS datasets across prediction horizons H ∈ {12, 24, 48, 96}. Red indicates first rank performance, Blue indicates the second rank. w/o A. Gates: Adaptive Gates
Table 8: Complexity and performance comparison between RhyMix and recent SOTA models. ↑ indicates the competitor model has more parameters than RhyMix.
